cheap_validate.result: GREEN end-to-end on real data (real fathomdb.rerank TinyBERT load + airlock gpt-5-nano reader)
cheap_validate.note: FAIL is EXPECTED at n=8 on a cheap reader (machinery validation only, not a result). The cheap run answers the reranked arm with gpt-5-nano while the reused fathomdb/mem0 cells were produced by gpt-5.4 — cross-reader, fine for a plumbing smoke; the PRICED full pass is identical-answerer (see below).

priced_full_command_EXACT: FATHOMDB_TESTS_NO_REBUILD=1 PYTHONPATH=src/python /home/coreyt/projects/fathomdb/.venv/bin/python -m eval.rerank_accuracy_run --mode full --d0b-checkpoint <CANONICAL 606-record gpt-5.4 0.8.3-d0b-parity-v2.checkpoint.json> --gap-checkpoint <gpt-5.4 gap-decomp per-question checkpoint OR omit> --opening-balance-usd <cumulative 0.8.3 spend to date> --output dev/plans/runs/0.8.3-rerank-accuracy-n606.json
priced_full_notes: full-mode reader defaults to gpt-5.4 (resolve_reader) — do NOT override; it MUST equal the reused-cell reader (the d0b-parity-v2 checkpoint reader = gpt-5.4) for the identical-answerer invariant.
Do NOT put any key on the CLI — the airlock base_url/key come from ELPS_LLM_BASE_URL/ELPS_LLM_API_KEY env (defaults localhost:4000).

~606 NEW gpt-5.4 reader calls (OpenAI, off the Google-503 path) ~= $3 of the remaining cap; set --opening-balance-usd to the true cumulative 0.8.3 spend so the $30 cap is per-PROGRAM. Resilience is wired: per-question checkpoint+auto-resume, pre-call $-cap (halt BEFORE the call), failure!=abstention, completeness citability gate.

The d0b-parity-v2 606-record checkpoint currently lives only in the slice-10-phaseB worktree (/tmp/fdb-slice-10-phaseB-20260622T010736Z/dev/plans/runs/); point --d0b-checkpoint at the canonical copy. load_reused_cells HARD-STOPS on an aggregate-only artifact (e.g. 0.8.3-d0b-parity-n606.json has NO per-question records).

--gap-checkpoint is OPTIONAL (only the non-gating oracle_headroom_captured needs it). A gpt-5.4 gap-decomp PER-QUESTION checkpoint may not exist (the published 0.8.3-gap-decomposition-n606.json is aggregate-only); if absent, omit --gap-checkpoint -> oracle_headroom_captured is None (gate unaffected). The gpt-5-nano gap cheapval used here is reader-mismatched and is for the cheap smoke only.
subtleties_for_review: AIRLOCK-503 RISK: N/A for this arm. The 503 risk flagged in the prompt is on the gap-decomp CHEAP DISTILLER (Google flash-lite) path; this arm has NO distiller and the cheap-validate uses gpt-5-nano (OpenAI). No Google-503 exposure.

RERANKED-vs-RAW TOP-K: test (b) test_answerer_sees_reranked_topk_not_raw_order PROVES the plumbing delivers the RERANKED order (not the raw fused order) as the answerer context. Whether the reranked top-K actually DIFFERS from FathomDB's raw top-K on real data (and thus can move accuracy) is what the PRICED pass measures; the cheap n=8 showed reranked acc == fathomdb acc (both 0.0) i.e. no realized lift at that tiny n/cheap reader.

IDENTICAL-ANSWERER: the gate is paired (fathomdb_reranked - fathomdb). fathomdb cells are REUSED from the gpt-5.4 d0b checkpoint; the priced reranked arm must also be gpt-5.4 (full default) so the pairing is same-reader. Cheap mode intentionally breaks this (smoke only).
EXTENSION: import fathomdb requires the gitignored .so symlink in src/python/fathomdb/ (created, NOT committed; no rebuild). Pure tests do not need it.
decide_rerank_accuracy GO uses gap_to_mem0_closed (literal (reranked-fathomdb)/(mem0-fathomdb)); a None gap (mem0 absent / mem0==fathomdb) can never be GO (never assumed).
